Article.ArticleTitle: Separation of per- and polyfluorinated alkyl substances (PFAS) by means of anion exchange chromatography and study of their retention behavior.

Abstract.AbstractText: The retention behavior of anionic per- and polyfluorinated alkyl substances (PFAS) on six anion exchange stationary phases with different construction principles, ion exchange capacities and polarities were investigated in order to derive their retention mechanisms in ion exchange chromatography. The chosen elution system based on water, acetonitrile and ammonium carbonate allows the variation of pH, polarity and ion exchange elution power under ESI-MS compatible conditions. Thus, perfluorinated alkyl acids show a separation behavior that is characterized by a mixture of electrostatic and hydrophobic interactions. Different selectivities were obtained for perfluorinated carboxylic, sulfonic and phosphonic acids, which correlate with their electrostatic properties such as their hydration sphere and net charge as well as their log P values. By varying the content of acetonitrile, the concentration of ammonium carbonate as well as the pH of the mobile phase, the retention of perfluorinated alkyl acids is influenced in different ways depending on their functional group and chain length. The retention of short-chain perfluorinated alkyl acids results primarily from electrostatic interactions via the polar head group, while the retention behavior of long-chain perfluorinated alkyl acids is mainly characterized by hydrophobic interactions. In contrast to conventional reversed phase liquid chromatography, ion exchange chromatography allows a systematic separation of perfluorinated alkyl acids according to both their chain length and functional group.
